text:
REGIME — Risk-on, small caps led, tech surged, defensives faded.

INDICES — VIX 19.1, falling; VIXY -3.80%. IWM +1.44%, QQQ +1.03%, SPY +0.98%, DIA +0.60% — small caps outperformed large caps across the board. USO surged +2.92%, the most notable cross-asset move, contradicting the truce-driven crude narrative.

SECTORS — XLK +2.10%, XLF +1.75%, XLY +0.91%, XLC +0.76%, XLI +0.71%, XLRE +0.47%, XLV +0.45%, XLB +0.44%, XLE +0.30%, XLP -1.00%, XLU -1.21%. Top industries: WGMI +5.85%, WCLD +5.44%. Bottom industries: SLV -1.16%, XLU -1.21%.

THE 7s — Software +5.46%, Cloud +4.61%, Toll Road +2.76%, New Metals +2.65%, Medtech +2.11%, Insurance +2.01%, Semi +1.91%, Buildout +1.59%, New Staples +1.55%, Bank +1.18%, Defense +1.00%, Fab +0.78%, Housing +0.72%, Industrial +0.42%, Energy +0.37%, Health +0.20%, Freight +0.09%, Power -0.59%, Old Staples -0.88%, Old Metals -0.91%. Strongest ticker in Software 7 led WGMI's +5.85% session; weakest in Old Metals 7 dragged SLV to -1.16%.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29, 2026; CME holds at 100% hold, 0% cut/hike. Fed stance: 6 hawks (Cook, Hammack, Kashkari, Logan, Barkin, Goolsbee), 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD Liquidity Stress 54/100 Elevated — Reserve Scarcity at 80th percentile is the most-stressed component; CP-Tbill Spread (79th percentile) is a close second; ON-RRP collapsed to $0.2B and TGA dropped $99.3B.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 60.6, Greed. Top movers: WGMI +5.85% (Industries), Software 7 +5.46% (Baskets), WCLD +5.44% (Industries). 20d momentum: best sector XLK +6.57%, worst XLP -3.22%; best basket Semi +22.48%, worst Software -10.88%. Middle East truce announcement drove the broad risk-on rally, lifting tech and small caps hardest.

WATCH — LIT +2.72% (3.0x volume spike today; unusual vol surge warrants watching for continuation or reversal). XLP -1.00% (worst sector 1d and -3.22% 20d; defensive breakdown accelerating against risk-on backdrop). XLE +0.30% (weakest cyclical despite USO +2.92% — severe divergence worth monitoring).
